needs_user_input.question: Should boundary-layer packages be exempt from INLINE_SIDE_EFFECT checks?
needs_user_input.context: 23 of 42 Go failures are in packages whose entire purpose IS the database/Firestore boundary: internal/state/ (13 failures), internal/events/store.go (5), internal/scheduler/lock.go (4), internal/monitor/business.go:toFloat64 (1). These functions ARE the named boundary — the checker flags them because it doesn't know they're the boundary layer.
options: A: Add a directive/annotation (e.g. `// dag:boundary`) to exempt these functions and update the checker
B: Add package-level exemptions to the checker config (e.g. internal/state, internal/events)
C: Restructure so each package has an explicit thin `repo.go` or `store.go` with ONLY raw DB calls, and other functions in the package call those
needs_user_input.recommendation: A — lowest churn. The functions are already well-structured boundary functions on receiver types. A directive marks intent explicitly for the static DAG agent.
affected_files: internal/state/firestore.go (9 — intervalsCol, MarkInProgress, MarkComplete, MarkFailed, RecoverOrphans, ListEvents, lockRef, AcquireLock, ReleaseLock)

needs_user_input.question: Should source package Firestore/GCS calls be restructured or exempted?
needs_user_input.context: internal/source/gcs_versioned.go has 6 failures across versionsCol, List, Register (file_io + GCS + Firestore), Activate. This is a boundary package that talks to GCS and Firestore as its core responsibility. Same question as INPUT-01 but for a multi-backend boundary.
options: A: Exempt with directive (same as INPUT-01)
B: Extract a thin GCSClient and FirestoreVersionStore that own raw calls, have gcs_versioned.go orchestrate through them
needs_user_input.recommendation: A for now, B later as a cleanup. Register() does file_io + GCS + Firestore in sequence which is a legitimate mixed-responsibility signal, but splitting it is a design task not a pre-check fix.
affected_files: internal/source/gcs_versioned.go (6 — versionsCol, List, Register x3, Activate)

needs_user_input.question: Fix scope: should examples/ and scripts/ be included in the fix pass?
needs_user_input.context: 9 Python failures are in examples/mixed_pipeline/ (2) and scripts/test_dlt_extract.py (6) and dag/scripts/check_codebase.py (1). These are tooling/example code, not the core pipeline.
options: A: Fix everything — examples should model best practices
B: Fix scripts/, skip examples/ — examples are illustrative, not production
C: Skip both — focus on internal/ and cmd/ only
needs_user_input.recommendation: B — scripts/ is operational code. Examples are documentation.

needs_user_input.question: SWALLOWED_ERROR in executePipeline: are these intentional fire-and-forget?
needs_user_input.context: cmd/granicus/serve.go:executePipeline has 7 swallowed errors. This is the main pipeline execution path in the server. Need to verify whether these are intentional 'log and continue' patterns or genuine gaps.
options: A: Audit each one and either handle or add // dag:intentional comment
B: Wrap all in a helper that logs + continues, making intent explicit
needs_user_input.recommendation: A — need to read each case.

fix_waves.name: Mechanical failures — clear fixes, no design decisions

items.fix: Extract the direct db.Exec/QueryRow calls into named boundary functions (e.g. insertMonitorSnapshot, createDatasetIfNotExists) in the same file or a nearby helper. The orchestrator calls the boundary function instead of inlining SQL.
This reads a SQL file from disk. Extract to a named function like readSQLFile() or loadCompletenessTemplate().
checkStateDB does stat + db ping inline. Extract fileExists() and pingDB() boundary helpers.
Extract file removal into a named boundary function (e.g. removeFile or removeOldArchives).
This IS a file I/O function — candidate for boundary exemption or rename to make boundary role explicit.
toFloat64 converts a BQ result to float64 — the DB call is a query execution. Extract to a named query function.
These make direct BQ API calls. Since testmode is a utility package (not the state layer), extract boundary functions for each BQ operation.
Extract file write into a named boundary function.

items.fix_if_A: Add // dag:boundary directive above each flagged function
items.fix_if_C: Extract raw Firestore calls to a firestoreRepo type, have FirestoreStateBackend call it
Extract raw SQL to a sqliteRepo type
Split into events/repo.go for raw SQL, events/store.go for orchestration
LockStore is already thin — exemption is the right call

items.fix_if_B: Extract GCSClient + FirestoreVersionStore, have gcs_versioned orchestrate

items.fix: Wrap module-level code in if __name__ == '__main__': guard or a main() function.
Add type annotations to all functions. Extract file write in write_metadata to a boundary.
Add return type annotation, extract file I/O.
Add godoc comments to all 185 exported functions. Pattern: '// FuncName does X.' on the line above. Can be done per-package in batches. This is the highest-count issue but purely mechanical.

functions: cmd/granicus/main.go:runRun (460 lines) — main pipeline execution, needs major decomposition
items.fix: Each needs individual decomposition. runRun is the priority — 460 lines with 8 params is a clear mixed-responsibility signal.
Many of these are 52-70 lines — borderline. Prioritize ones that also have other warnings (HIGH_PARAM_COUNT, SWALLOWED_ERROR).
Mostly in dag/scripts/ tooling. Lower priority than Go core.

Audit each. Many are likely regex patterns, error sentinels, or default configs that should be const or passed as params. Regexes compiled at package level are acceptable (const-like). True mutable state should be refactored to pass through constructors.
Introduce options/config structs for functions with >5 params. Group related params. Priority: runDryRun(8), buildRunJSON(8), executePipeline(6+).
Audit each. Options: (a) handle the error, (b) add // dag:intentional if fire-and-forget is correct, (c) propagate. executePipeline in serve.go has 7 alone — see INPUT-04.
Review cmd/granicus/serve.go:init, internal/runner/gcs_ingest_runner.go:init, internal/server/metrics.go:init. Move init logic to explicit constructor calls where possible.
Add docstrings to all flagged Python functions. Mechanical.
Warning only. These are dag/scripts/ tooling functions that log as part of their job. No action needed unless you want structured logging.
Informational only. These feed into the static DAG as boundary markers. No code fix needed.

execution_order: 1. Get user input on INPUT-01 through INPUT-04
2. Wave 1 — mechanical failure fixes (13 items, no dependencies)
3. Wave 2 — boundary exemptions based on INPUT-01/02 answer (29 items)
4. Wave 3 — Python failures based on INPUT-03 answer (9 items)
5. Wave 4 — MISSING_GODOC bulk pass (185 items, can be parallelized by package)
6. Wave 5 — HIGH_LINE_COUNT decomposition (81 items, start with critical 6)
7. Wave 6 — structural quality fixes (75 items, case-by-case)
8. Wave 7 — Python docs + skip logging/boundary markers (55 items, mostly skip)
